news.summary: A new Skechers store, the first in Europe to focus on performance products, has recently opened in Belgium. This marks a significant milestone for the popular footwear brand, showcasing its commitment to the European market. The store is located in Waalwijk, Netherlands, and aims to cater to customers seeking high-performance footwear. This strategic move reflects Skechers' dedication to providing specialized products to meet the needs of athletes and individuals looking for top-quality athletic shoes. The opening of this store highlights the brand's emphasis on expanding its presence in Europe and offering specialized products to a growing customer base.
news.headline: Europe's first performance-focused Skechers store opens in Belgium, located in WAALWIJK, Netherlands.

news.summary: Hugel Inc., a major global medical aesthetics company based in SEOUL, South Korea, announced its financial results for the first quarter of 2025. The company reported impressive growth with KRW89.8 billion in net sales, KRW39.0 billion in operating profit, and KRW30.9 billion in net profit. These figures represent significant increases of 20.9%, 62.6%, and 35.9% year-over-year, respectively. The growth was driven by strong performances across all product lines including botulinum toxin, dermal fillers, and cosmetics. Hugel Inc. is expected to continue its strong global performance, with plans for additional shipments to the US in the second quarter. Suk Cha serves as the chairman of the company.
news.headline: Hugel records record Q1 earnings with KRW89.8B net sales, KRW39.0B operating profit, and KRW30.9B net profit; up 20.9%, 62.6%, and 35.9% on-year.

news.summary: Expanding the SiConic Ecosystem to support test engineers throughout the development lifecycle, from structural test initiation to ATE and SLT deployment. This extension aims to empower test engineers at various stages, enhancing efficiency and accuracy.

news.summary: In London, Ravio has secured a $12 million Series A funding round, spearheaded by Spark Capital (US) and joined by Blackbird (Australia) and Cherry Ventures (Europe). The funding aims to revolutionize how businesses handle compensation using real-time market data and decision-making tools. Ravio boasts a clientele including major consumer brands like Just Eat Takeaway and Octopus Energy, as well as fintech giants Wise, Adyen, and Mollie. Noteworthy tech players such as Zoopla and Skyscanner have also opted for Ravio's services.
news.headline: Ravio secures $12m Series A funding to revamp compensation data, with big clients like Just Eat and Wise.

news.summary: NXP Semiconductors N.V. launched its latest S32R47 imaging radar processors in Eindhoven, The Netherlands, on May 8, 2025. The processors, manufactured using 16 nm FinFET technology, are a significant advancement in the imaging radar sector. This new generation offers double the processing power compared to its predecessor, while also enhancing system cost and power efficiency. When paired with NXP’s mmWave radar transceivers, power management, and in-vehicle networking solutions, the S32R47 family complies with functional safety ASIL ISO 26262 ASIL B(D) standards. This release positions the automotive industry for enhanced levels of autonomous driving capabilities. The company's stock, listed as NASDAQ: NXPI, is likely to see a notable reaction following this announcement.
news.headline: NXP launches third-gen S32R47 imaging radar processors, offering double processing power in 16nm technology, for Level 2+ to 4 autonomous driving.

news.summary: AMC Entertainment Holdings Inc. (NYSE: AMC) remains confident despite a challenging first quarter. CEO Adam Aron acknowledged the company's struggles with box office collections hitting their lowest point for a non-COVID period since 1996. Aron described the period as unusually slow but believes it is an anomaly rather than a long-term trend. Looking ahead, AMC is optimistic about the rest of the year, expecting strong demand for theatrical releases. Aron stated that movie-going demand will showcase significant growth for the remainder of 2025 and throughout 2026. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: AMC CEO Adam Aron optimistic despite worst Q1 box office since 1996. Predicts rebound in demand for movies by 2025.

news.summary: Carvana Co. (NYSE: CVNA) surpassed expectations in their first-quarter results, achieving an 11.5% adjusted EBITDA margin, exceeding their long-term profitability goal years ahead of schedule. CEO Ernie Garcia pointed out that their initial target was projected in 2018 for a later period, and the company has consistently operated within the anticipated margin range of 8% to 13.5%. Moreover, Garcia suggested that tariffs might have a positive impact on the used car market compared to new vehicles, which could see price hikes. The CEO alluded to potential benefits for used car pricing amidst tariff influences.
news.headline: Carvana reports Q1 results exceeding expectations, achieves 11.5% adjusted EBITDA margin. CEO believes tariffs may benefit used cars.

news.summary: In 2024, NRD Cyber Security experienced notable success with substantial growth, cutting-edge advancements, and expansion on a global scale. The firm achieved a consolidated revenue of EUR 10,194 million during the year, marking a substantial upsurge of 37.6% from the previous year. Furthermore, the net profit surged by 23.3% to EUR 1,012 million in the same period.
news.headline: NRD Cyber Security's revenue grew by 37.6% to EUR 10,194 million in 2024, with net profit up by 23.3% to EUR 1,012 million.

news.summary: Shopify Inc. (NASDAQ: SHOP) is set to reveal its first-quarter earnings on May 8 before the market opens. Analysts are anticipating earnings of 26 cents per share, an increase from 20 cents per share in the same period last year. The company is expected to report quarterly revenue of $2.33 billion, up from $1.86 billion a year ago. Shopify recently announced an advancement in its global expansion plans in partnership with Affirm Holdings, Inc. (NASDAQ: AFRM) on April 9. Closing at $94.50 on Wednesday, Shopify's shares witnessed a 0.7% increase. Further details and analyst ratings can be accessed through Benzinga Pro.
news.headline: Shopify gearing up for Q1 earnings, expected to report 26 cents per share on $2.33 billion revenue projections; shares rose 0.7% to $94.50. - approx. 101 characters

news.summary: Faraday Future Intelligent Electric Inc. (NASDAQ: FFAI), a California-based global company, has submitted three patent applications through its subsidiary, Future AIHER, to lead in intelligent electric powertrain innovation. The subsidiary, launched in March 2025, is the first AI hybrid extended-range electric vehicle company.
news.headline: Faraday Future files 3 patents via Future AIHER for AI-powered hybrid systems, aiming for leadership in electric powertrain innovation.

news.summary: In recent data released by the authorities, it was reported that the number of new cases of COVID-19 has increased by 15% compared to last week, with a total of 10,000 new cases confirmed. Additionally, the number of deaths related to the virus has also risen by 8%, reaching a total of 500 fatalities. The spike in cases has prompted officials to reinforce safety measures and urge the public to adhere to social distancing guidelines to curb the spread of the virus. The healthcare system is currently under strain due to the surge in cases, with hospitalizations up by 20% in the past week. Authorities are closely monitoring the situation and are considering implementing stricter restrictions if the situation continues to escalate.
news.headline: German industrial output rose by 2.6% in May despite looming US tariffs -Bloomberg.
Trump to unveil trade agreement with UK soon.

news.summary: In a recent report, it has been revealed that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 5.2%, marking a significant decrease from the previous quarter's rate of 6.1%. The total number of unemployed individuals now stands at 1.5 million. Additionally, the report noted a growth in the number of new jobs created, with an increase of 250,000 jobs during the same period. This data indicates positive trends in the labor market and suggests an improving economic outlook for the country.
news.headline: Federal Reserve faces stagflation concerns amidst ongoing uncertainties. Meeting focuses on stabilizing economy, with inflation at 5.4%.

news.summary: David Bailey, CEO of Bitcoin Magazine and cryptocurrency advisor to President Donald Trump, has successfully raised a total of $300 million for the establishment of a publicly traded Bitcoin investment company. This fundraising effort, initiated in January, comprised $200 million in equity funding and $100 million in convertible debt, as revealed by sources familiar with the matter. The newly formed company, Nakamoto, pays tribute to the pseudonymous Bitcoin creator Satoshi Nakamoto, and its main objective will involve the acquisition and long-term holding of Bitcoin. Nakamoto is anticipated to merge with an existing Nasdaq-listed firm, with an official announcement slated for early next week. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Trump crypto advisor David Bailey raised a total of $300 million for Nakamoto, a publicly traded Bitcoin investment company, with $200 million in equity funding and $100 million in convertible debt. Nakamoto is expected to merge with a Nasdaq-listed company soon.

news.summary: The article reports on the financial performance of a company in the first quarter of 2025. Total sales reached MSEK 39,606, with a 3% growth in organic sales. Operating income before amortization stood at MSEK 2,525 with an operating margin of 6.4%. Earnings per share increased to SEK 2.29. The net debt/EBITDA ratio improved to 2.5. Cash flow from operating activities also showed an increase. The company's resilient business model was highlighted, especially in navigating global uncertainty and geopolitical risks. The firm's focus on technology and solutions services led to a 5% growth in that segment. The company remains vigilant about monitoring developments in the market and maintaining a strong balance sheet.
news.headline: Securitas AB Q1 2025: Total sales MSEK 39,606, operating margin 6.4%, earnings per share SEK 2.36, with 3% organic sales growth.

news.summary: Singapore-based company Hafnia Limited, listed on the Oslo Stock Exchange with ticker code "HAFNI" and on the New York Stock Exchange with ticker code "HAFN", is set to announce its Q1 2025 financial results at 07:30 CET on May 15, 2025. The company will host an investor presentation featuring CEO Mikael Skov, CFO Perry van Echtelt, VP Søren Skibdal Winther, and EVP Thomas Andersen. The event will take place on the same day at different local times: 14:30 CET in Oslo, Norway, and earlier in New York.
news.headline: Hafnia to release Q1 2025 results on May 15, 2025, with an investor presentation featuring CEO, CFO, VP, and EVP.

news.summary: Robinhood is reportedly developing a blockchain network to allow retail investors in Europe to trade US securities through tokenized assets like stocks. This move aims to expand the company's local presence and reduce costs associated with traditional financial infrastructure. Robinhood acquired a brokerage license in Lithuania in April 2025 and has entered a deal to purchase crypto exchange Bitstamp. Robinhood's shares rose 2.7% on May 7 on Nasdaq, and its revenue fell 8.6% in Q1 2025 but exceeded Wall Street's estimates. The project partners, Arbitrum and the Solana Foundation, have yet to finalize agreements. Traditional financial firms like Banco Santander and JP Morgan are also exploring blockchain solutions. Magazine "Ethereum is destroying the competition in the $16.1T TradFi tokenization race" reflects the market's growing interest in blockchain-based advancements.
news.headline: Robinhood plans blockchain for US asset trading in Europe: Sources say, tokenizing stocks for faster transactions. Two crypto firms vying to partner.

news.summary: Binance founder Changpeng Zhao, also known as CZ, revealed that he applied for a pardon from US President Donald Trump, following reports in March suggesting he was seeking one amid discussions of a Trump family business deal with Binance.US. Zhao stated on a podcast that he had his lawyers file the application two weeks prior, influenced by Trump's pardon of three BitMEX founders. Zhao pleaded guilty to a money laundering charge in November 2023 as part of a $4.3 billion deal Binance made with US authorities. Despite the pardon not erasing his conviction, it could potentially allow him to take on a management role at Binance.US. Since stepping down as CEO, Zhao has expressed no intent to regain the position, focusing on advisory roles related to crypto regulation and blockchain solutions in countries like Pakistan and Kyrgyzstan.
news.headline: Binance founder Changpeng Zhao applies for a US pardon post money laundering conviction, submits request 2 weeks ago. Source denies earlier reports. Zhao contributed $50 million to a $4.3 billion fine, served four months in prison, and stepped down as CEO. Pardon won't erase conviction but may allow him a role at Binance.US. Zhao remains a shareholder but has no plans to return as CEO. Involved in advisory roles in Pakistan and Kyrgyzstan post-prison release. Magazine discusses Adam Back and Blockstream theories.

news.summary: In Gothenburg, Sweden, on May 8, 2025, Zinzino's shareholders approved the subscription of 751,617 B shares in the company's option programme on May 15, 2020. Each share was priced at SEK 45, resulting in a total equity increase of SEK 33,822,765 for Zinzino.
news.headline: Zinzino AB raised SEK 33,822,765 by issuing 751,617 B shares at SEK 45/share through a share subscription.
UK Defense Secretary Healey: Declines comment on US trade talks.

news.summary: The Binance-affiliated BNB Chain has experienced a rebound in 2023 due to increasing institutional and DeFi adoption. BNB Chain saw multibillion-dollar inflows into DeFi and ETFs, leading its native token BNB to surpass all-time highs in the first quarter of 2025, trading at around $600 with a market cap of nearly $85 billion. Standard Chartered predicts BNB to reach $2,775 by 2028. BNB Chain's TVL has increased from $3.5 billion to over $6 billion, ranking fourth among layer-1 blockchain networks. Its use cases are primarily on DEXs, lending protocols, and liquid staking. Binance's ties have driven institutional adoption, with the exchange accounting for 40% of global spot trading volumes and issuing the USD1 stablecoin primarily on the BNB Chain. More than $2 billion worth of USD1 has been issued on BNB Chain, representing 99% of its circulating supply.
news.headline: BNB Chain rebounds with rising DeFi and institutional adoption, trading at $600 with $85B market cap. BNB expected to hit $2,775 by 2028.

news.summary: The US Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) has given banks under its jurisdiction the green light to trade cryptocurrency on behalf of customers, as per a letter issued by acting comptroller Rodney Hood on May 7. Banks can buy and sell crypto held in custody for customers, outsource crypto activities to third parties, as well as offer custody, tax, and reporting services for crypto assets. The OCC's move follows a previous easing of restrictions in March regarding crypto-asset custody and stablecoin activities. Over 50 million Americans hold crypto, signaling a shift towards digitalized financial services. The OCC's decision has been welcomed by industry experts, with general counsel Katherine Kirkpatrick Bos and Chief Policy Officer at Coinbase Faryar Shirzad praising the regulatory clarity provided. Additionally, the Trump administration has displayed a more favorable stance towards cryptocurrency since coming to power in January, as evidenced by the withdrawal of guidance by the US Federal Reserve and the reversal of a Biden-era rule on decentralized finance protocol reporting.
news.headline: US banks under OCC can trade customer crypto assets, outsource crypto activities. More than 50M Americans hold cryptocurrency. Regulatory clarity provided. Trump admin has friendly stance on crypto. Fed withdraws guidance to deter bank involvement in crypto activities. Biden-era rule on DeFi reporting transactions to IRS overturned. SEC's change in approach to crypto raises questions.

news.summary: ZachXBT, an onchain analyst, has identified that $45 million has been stolen from Coinbase users through social engineering scams in just the past seven days. This represents part of a larger trend where $330 million is estimated to be lost annually by these users. While Coinbase has not responded to these claims, it is noted that this issue seems to be unique to this particular exchange. The increasing sophistication of these scams has caught the attention of the FBI, who have issued warnings about scammers pretending to be crypto exchanges to steal funds and sensitive data. These scams have taken various forms, including fake support staff and malicious software disguised as job opportunities. There have also been reports of scam emails impersonating legitimate exchanges and directing users to transfer their funds externally. In response, Coinbase's chief security officer has called for a unified framework to combat these scams effectively.
news.headline: $45M stolen from Coinbase users last week, totaling $330M annually in social engineering scams. FBI warns of increasing attacks.
